commands_executed: bash -c 'while IFS=: read -r user _ uid _ _ home shell; do if [ "$uid" -eq 0 ] || [ "$uid" -ge 1000 ]; then case "$user" in nobody|nfsnobody) continue;; esac; if [[ "$shell" != *nologin && "$shell" != *false && -d "$home" ]]; then for f in .bashrc .bash_profile .profile .bash_logout .cshrc .tcshrc .kshrc .zshrc .zprofile .zlogin .zshenv .login; do if [ -f "$home/$f" ]; then chmod 0740 "$home/$f"; fi; done; fi; fi; done < /etc/passwd'

execution_details.command: bash -c 'while IFS=: read -r user _ uid _ _ home shell; do if [ "$uid" -eq 0 ] || [ "$uid" -ge 1000 ]; then case "$user" in nobody|nfsnobody) continue;; esac; if [[ "$shell" != *nologin && "$shell" != *false && -d "$home" ]]; then for f in .bashrc .bash_profile .profile .bash_logout .cshrc .tcshrc .kshrc .zshrc .zprofile .zlogin .zshenv .login; do if [ -f "$home/$f" ]; then chmod 0740 "$home/$f"; fi; done; fi; fi; done < /etc/passwd'

scan_output: Vulnerability file_permission_user_init_files_root not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message: The remediation was applied successfully. The script iterated through all interactive users (UID 0 or ≥ 1000), excluded `nobody` and `nfsnobody`, ensured each user’s shell is interactive, and set the mode of any existing initialization files (e.g., `.bashrc`, `.profile`, `.bash_profile`, etc.) to **0740**. The command exited with status 0 and produced no errors, bringing the system into compliance with the OpenSCAP rule **file_permission_user_init_files_root**.
